LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS EDWARD JONG, GOLDEN PACIFIC REALTY AND INVESTMENT CO. MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: MOTION DENIED, FAILURE TO SHIFT THE BURDEN OF PROOF. PLAINTIFF ALLEGES THAT DEFENDANT BREACHED A WRITTEN CONTRACT AS WELL AS ORAL AGREEMENTS. THE ORAL AGREEMENTS REGARDED VARIOUS SPECIFICS ABOUT HOW THE WORK WAS TO BE CARRIED OUT AND PLAINTIFF PERFORMED ACCORDINGLY. FURTHERMORE, TRIABLE ISSUE OF FACT EXISTS WHETHER THE PARTIES MADE A NEW, SEPARATE ORAL AGREEMENT, OR IF THEY ORALLY MODIFIED THE ORIGINAL AGREEMENT. JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE CSR #5797

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Jun-03-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANTS EDWARD JONG, GOLDEN PACIFIC REALTY AND INVESTMENT CO. MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. Appearances: Robert Garcia, Esq. Attorney at Law, (415) 648-3060 appearing for Plaintiff and opposing party; Mattaniah Eytan, Esq. Law Offices of Mattaniah Eytan (415) 399-1000 appearing for Defendants and Moving party. Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Motion denied, failure to shift the burden of proof. Plaintiff alleges that defendant breached a written contract as well as oral agreements. The oral agreements regarded various specifics about how the work was to be carried out and plaintiff performed accordingly. Furthermore, triable issue of fact exists whether the parties made a new, separate oral agreement, or if they orally modified the original agreement. (302/CWW) Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard; Court Reporter: Kent Gubbine, CSR #5797
